_comment: Pronunciation overrides for Romanian TTS. These ALIAS respellings are sent to ElevenLabs INSTEAD of the original word; UI text is never changed. They target Romanian words spelled like English words, which an English-biased voice may anglicize. Trick: respell phonetically and break the English spelling (hyphen splits a syllable; 'k' forces a hard c). Read under the ro-enforced voice these produce the correct Romanian sound. Run: npm run audio:overrides:apply
_respelling_key: a=ah, e=eh, i=ee, o=oh, u=oo, c+a/o/u=k, c+e/i=ch, g+e/i=j(soft). A hyphen only splits syllables to break the English word-match; it does not change the sounds.
_warning_common_words: Entries like 'are', 'care', 'mare', 'sale', 'date' are VERY common and appear inside full sentences too. The override applies globally, so they get respelled everywhere. If you hear odd micro-pauses inside sentences, delete those entries and rely on language enforcement for in-sentence context (surrounding Romanian words usually fix detection anyway) — keep overrides mainly for isolated single-word audio.
_caveat_monosyllables: Single-syllable false friends (bun, un, nu, plan) are hard to alias because Romanian has no vowel-doubling and 'h' is voiced, so respellings backfire. These rely on language enforcement; verify them by ear rather than forcing a respelling.
_how_to_add: Add 'word': 'respelling' to 'words', then run: npm run audio:overrides:apply
